title: pavonia aschersonioides
intro: Pavonia aschersonioides, also known as the Brazilian rose mallow or wild hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use in wound healing and skin care. The herbal preparations of Pavonia aschersonioides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers of the plant in hot water or a solvent.
